name: sidebar
description: The application sidebar rendered server-side: a <nav> landmark of real <a href> nav items (groups -> menu-items -> collapsible sub-items via native <details>), aria-current on the active entry, plus a collapse/expand rail and a mobile off-canvas overlay. Server-first PARTIAL + a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (ADR-0012, Wave 3); replaces the <lv-sidebar> Lit island. The nav is server-rendered (which item is current is a server fact); the collapse is pure client cosmetic with localStorage persistence, never a wire round-trip. shadcn Sidebar anatomy (header/content/footer, group/menu/menu-sub), WAI-ARIA navigation landmark.
docs: Copies the sidebar partial to your JTE root at lievit/sidebar.jte (reached as @template.lievit.sidebar(...)), its sub-partials at lievit/sidebar/{group,item}.jte (@template.lievit.sidebar.group / .item), and the CSP-clean enhancer to your alias root at lievit/sidebar.enhancer.ts. Compose the nav from your typed model: a sidebar.group per section, a sidebar.item per entry (key, label, href, icon, badge, active, disabled; pass a `content` sub-tree to make an item a collapsible parent). Set each item's `active` from the route being rendered so it carries aria-current=page. Import the enhancer once from your entry module and call enhanceAllSidebars() on load (and after any DOM swap); it toggles the desktop collapsed rail (persisted under storageKey via localStorage) and the mobile off-canvas overlay. The source is yours: edit freely.
